output: Completed batch 4 tasks T13 and T14. Wired `AgentChat` into `VideoEditorShell` behind a top-bar toggle as an absolute right-side drawer overlay so it floats over the editor without displacing the properties panel or changing the existing realtime conflict-dialog path. Tightened the remaining failure handling in the timeline agent and session hook: Groq calls now retry once before surfacing a user-visible waiting-state error message, invalid tool-argument JSON is returned as a tool result for self-correction, and the client hook now tracks retryable send failures and exposes retry state that the chat UI renders. Verified the touched files with targeted ESLint, `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false`, and `git diff --check`, and wrote the batch checkpoint.
deviations: Did not run a live browser interaction for the floating drawer or a live Supabase/Groq invocation for the new failure paths in this batch. Verification is static: code inspection plus targeted ESLint, typecheck, and whitespace checks.

Advisory audit finding: Sense check SC15 is missing an executor acknowledgment.

task_updates.executor_notes: Integrated `AgentChat` into `VideoEditorShell` with a `MessageSquare` toggle in the top-right editor actions and an absolutely positioned ~350px right-side drawer overlay. The drawer uses opacity/translate transitions and does not alter the main grid layout, so the properties panel remains mounted underneath rather than being replaced. Verification: the existing `useTimelineRealtime` conflict-dialog wiring was left intact, targeted ESLint passed, `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` exited 0, and `git diff --check` passed for `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx`.

Completed the remaining edge/client failure handling. In `ai-timeline-agent`, Groq completions now retry once before appending a user-visible assistant error and returning `waiting_user`, invalid tool-argument JSON is surfaced as a `tool_result`, and the existing cancel/version-conflict/session-not-found paths were preserved. In the client send hook, retryable local send errors are tracked along with the last submitted message, and the chat UI now renders a Retry button when the last send failed or the session is in `error`. Verification: targeted ESLint passed, `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` exited 0, and `git diff --check` passed for `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/index.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.ts`, and the supporting `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx` UI update.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: The chat drawer is rendered as an absolutely positioned overlay inside `VideoEditorShell` rather than a new grid column, so it floats over the editor and does not displace the properties panel. The realtime conflict-dialog path was not changed in this batch; `useTimelineRealtime` is still wired exactly as before, so agent-originated remote writes still go through the existing dirty-edit conflict behavior. This was verified by code inspection plus type/lint checks, not by a live browser run.

Groq completion calls now retry once and, on second failure, append a user-visible assistant error message before the session exits with `status='waiting_user'` rather than failing silently. The loop still checks `timeline_agent_sessions.status` before each LLM turn and before each tool execution, so a cancelled session exits on the next turn boundary without continuing work. Verification here is static; I did not trigger a live Groq failure or cancellation race against a deployed edge function in this batch.
